threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think it's a 'quick walk' to the bus stop when it's really a 20-minute trek in heels. Good luck with that!
Caught the N42 last night and heard two lads arguing about whether you can buy a proper meat pie at 3 AM. Absolute madness.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a street artist painting a mural under the U-Bahn, while a kid sold homemade lemonade next to a guy juggling fire. That’s Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Last week, I saw a guy in a bathrobe at the U-Bahn station. Only in Kreuzberg would that be a normal morning commute.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a real local by their instinct to avoid BART delays; they’ll always suggest a bus route like it’s a secret shortcut nobody knows.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy trying to fix the Muni bus stop sign with duct tape. Smiled when he got a round of applause as the bus finally arrived. Only in SF.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about the hip bars, but the real charm is in the quiet corners where old folks still gossip over café con leche.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a newcomer by their struggle with the metro map, fumbling through their phones while locals glide past effortlessly.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still avoid the pavement when a dog’s on a lead, while locals casually sidestep like it’s a game of Frogger.
Caught a glimpse of a cat being walked on a leash at 2am near Brixton Market. Only in South London, right? Cat was clearly running the show.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I found an old lady feeding the pigeons in the park while arguing with a mural about her ex. That's Neukölln for you.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain can you find a bar with a pet pig as the mascot. It lounges freely, soaking in the attention while the beer flows.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a local if they know the best spot to grab a burrito after 2 AM and if they side-eye tourists trying to navigate the bus map.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 47 this morning and a guy in full clown attire got on, juggling while the bus lurched. Only in SF, right?

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think they can just hop on any tram and end up at Parc Güell. Meanwhile, locals know the best views are from the secret spots off Carrer de Progrés.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by the way they struggle with the siesta schedule; it’s 4 PM and they're still looking for lunch.
